{
  "analysis": "I need to rethink the approach. Let me examine specific frames from the HQ set (5fps, 951 frames) which provides better temporal resolution. I should check frames at key intervals to see all commands that appear over time. The 1fps set only has 190 frames, but the HQ set has 951 frames covering the same 190 seconds. At 5fps, I can see the typing animation more clearly and better detect when a command is completed.",
  "plan": "Run a quick test on a subset of HQ frames to verify OCR accuracy, then write a proper extraction script that processes frames and extracts commands more accurately.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "python3 << 'EOF'
import os
import subprocess

# Test with a few frames from HQ set to verify OCR
frames_dir = "/tmp/frames_hq"

# Check frames around 1.5s to 3s (frames 7-15) where first command 'n' is typed
for i in range(7, 20):
    f = f"frame_{i:04d}.png"
    result = subprocess.run(["tesseract", os.path.join(frames_dir, f), "stdout"], capture_output=True, text=True)
    text = result.stdout
    lines = text.split('\n')
    print(f"\n=== {f} ===")
    for line in lines:
        if line.strip().startswith('>'):
            print(f"  {line.strip()}")
EOF
",
      "duration": 10.0
    }
  ],
  "task_complete": false
}
